_publications._publicationTitle: CDK4/6 inhibition enhances SHP2 inhibitor efficacy and is dependent upon RB function in malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors

details: JH-2-002 MPNST cell line used extensively for mechanistic studies; TNO155 (SHP2i) suppressed proliferation and induced RB hypo-phosphorylation; E7-mediated RB inactivation caused ~5-fold increase in TNO155 IC50, confirming RB dependency; TNO155 + ribociclib (CDK4/6i) showed additive antiproliferative activity with greater suppression of pRB, cyclin E/A, PLK1, and IAPs (survivin, claspin), plus caspase-3/7 activation; RNA-seq (24 h) showed 2,503 differentially expressed genes with combination vs. 740 (TNO155 alone) or 1,277 (ribociclib alone)
